rows.what_you_get: A read-only/idempotent tool call can be served from a content-addressed local cache instead of re-executing, with automatic invalidation when the world changes.
gaps: Measured real-world hit-rate on tau2-airline is ~0.7% — far below a useful threshold, so the vDSO is an UPSIDE secondary, never the headline (the hit-rate is SIMULATED in the demo)
No standalone surface a person runs; it is a mechanism inside the syscall path
rows.durability_note: The 3-tier mechanism is deterministic and unit-proven; a cache hit equals a fresh call by construction (a write bumps the world-version).

rows.what_you_get: Run a frozen trace through the binary and watch in-process adjudication (~µs) vs a spawned-hook baseline (~ms) — a regression sentinel that the decide path never pays a per-call process boundary.
rows.witness: report.json gate_primary=='pass'; ~2.4 us in-process vs ~6.9 ms spawned (n=100)
gaps: Deliberately NOT a production-readiness, model-quality, or serving-throughput headline — a subsystem regression sentinel only
The ratio is platform-specific (the spawn baseline is this machine)
rows.durability_note: The gate is mechanical (in-process < spawn by construction); the vDSO hit-rate can never turn it red.

rows.concept: Turn-tax benchmark (the round-trip that never fires)
rows.what_you_get: Replay a real tool-call trace and count the extra error-recovery model round-trips a naive/tuned loop is forced into that the kernel resolves in the same syscall — with the safety floor on a separate axis.
gaps: The replayed trace is a cache-favorable slice; do not extrapolate the extra-turn count as a general agent speedup
The baseline turn cost is modeled; the fak side is live kernel counters
rows.durability_note: The fak side is live kernel events (k.Syscall counters), not modeled; a clean control saves exactly 0 (anti-inflation guard).

rows.what_you_get: Sweep one master goal into N sub-agents and measure the cross-agent tool-result dedup and shared-prefix KV reuse the fan-out buys — the regime no public multi-agent benchmark maps (they top out at 5-7).
gaps: The token-multiplier / tax-clawed-back values are a transparent SIMULATED cost model, reported apart from the measured dedup halves
N=1 is a net loss; the win is vs a stack that re-sends the prefix per sub-agent, NOT vs a tuned shared-prefix engine
rows.durability_note: The measured cross-agent dedup is a real shared-vs-isolated path swap; the (N-1)*prefix geometry is exact, proven bit-identical.

rows.what_you_get: Compute the exact, contention-free reread-elimination work floor for the >100k-token regime as closed-form arithmetic from the session shape — no GPU, no live model required.
gaps: The absolute WALL-CLOCK validation at >100k is SIMULATED (intractable on CPU; awaits a resident-GPU bench node, #524) — only the arithmetic floor is shipped
Decode-batching bandwidth win is excluded to isolate reread-only
rows.durability_note: The token floor is byte-identical to sessionbench's prefillTokens (cross-validation); the FLOP floor counts the prefill quadratic exactly — both deterministic and model-independent.

rows.concept: Shared-prefix KV reuse (prefill once, clone bit-identically)
rows.what_you_get: The kernel prefills a shared prompt prefix once and clones the KV bit-identically into every agent, so a read-heavy fleet does the shared setup work one time, not per agent per turn.
rows.witness: TestPrefixReuseFanoutWitness, NewBatchFromPrefix clones bit-identical to an independent full prefill
gaps: The fleet-scale benefit depends on read-heavy shared-context workloads; a ~1% write rate can flip it net-negative
Cross-engine KV share (vLLM/SGLang) is a seam, not shipped
rows.durability_note: The bit-identical clone is tested against a reference full-prefill; the (N-1)*prefix geometry is exact, model-independent arithmetic.

rows.what_you_get: A base-url-swappable OpenAI-compatible client with bounded timeout/backoff, cassette record/replay for deterministic offline runs, and a deterministic mock engine.
rows.witness: engine tests (units 39-45); deterministic mock + cassette replay
gaps: metrics-service scrape / KV-residency / token-per-watt are SIMULATED telemetry (no watt source on the box)
A library the gateway/agent consume, not a standalone surface
rows.durability_note: Cassette replay makes offline runs deterministic and CI-stable; a live run carries a real transcript hash XOR an explicit unverified flag.
